title: Final Fantasy XIV Fans Demand Butt Sliders — And Square Enix Says 'Not Yet'
summary: If you've ever played Final Fantasy XIV, you know that creating your character is a big deal. But there's one customization option that players have been craving for years: butt sliders. Yes, you read that right. At the annual Fan Festival in Anaheim, Square Enix revealed a slew of new features coming to the MMORPG, including the Evercold expansion in January and a crossover raid with Neon Genesis Evangelion. But when director Naoki Yoshida (affectionately known as Yoshi-P) took the stage to discuss character creator updates, he dropped a bombshell. His slide listed new color selectors for hair, eyes, and skin tone, face paint layering, and improved eye and lip customization. Then, with a straight face, he said: "No butt sliders... yet." The audience erupted in hoots, hollers, and laughter. One fan captured the moment on Twitter, captioning it "BUTT SLIDERS CONFIRMED???" The "yet" is crucial — it suggests Square Enix is at least considering the feature. While the current character creator is limited, the game's fashion system is top-notch. Many players, including this reporter, have been hoping for better options like more facial hair. If butt sliders eventually arrive — perhaps with the Evercold expansion — it could be a game-changer for character personalization. For now, fans will have to wait and see if Yoshi-P delivers on that tantalizing "yet."
why_it_matters: This debate highlights how game developers balance creative vision with player demands, and it shows that even 'silly' requests can reflect deeper desires for personal expression and representation in virtual worlds — an issue that affects how teens and adults alike shape their digital identities.

What did the audience do when Yoshi-P said 'No butt sliders... yet'?
What is the name of the fan who captured the moment on Twitter?

background_read: Final Fantasy XIV (an MMORPG launched by Square Enix in 2013 after a troubled initial version) allows players to customize their characters' race, face, and body but has never included body sliders for proportions like buttocks.

Square Enix (the Japanese video game company behind the Final Fantasy series) regularly holds Fan Festivals worldwide where directors like Naoki Yoshida announce major updates and gauge player reactions.

Character customization is a key selling point in MMORPGs; for example, World of Warcraft introduced new customization options in recent expansions, fueling demand in other games. The 'butt slider' request reflects a broader trend of players wanting more control over avatar body shapes.
Neon Genesis Evangelion is a classic 1990s Japanese anime about mecha and psychological struggle; its inclusion as a crossover raid shows how FFXIV mixes pop culture elements into its universe.
Article_Structure: LEAD: Hook about craving a specific customization option for years
EVENT: Square Enix's Fan Festival in Anaheim reveals new features
├─ NEW CONTENT: Evercold expansion, Evangelion crossover raid
└─ CHARACTER CREATOR UPDATES: color selectors, face paint layering, etc.
CLIMAX: Yoshi-P's slide says 'No butt sliders... yet'
└─ TWITTER MOMENT: fan caption 'BUTT SLIDERS CONFIRMED???'
ANALYSIS: 'Yet' suggests Square Enix is considering it
CLOSE: Wait-and-see conclusion with hopeful tone

perspectives.description: Players excited about potential customization see this as a win for personalization; more options mean avatars can better reflect real body types, which can be empowering and inclusive.

Some criticize the focus on such a niche feature as a waste of developer resources that could be used for story content or gameplay balance. Others worry about 'slippery slope' concerns over sexualization.

The request shows a pattern in MMORPG communities where players push for increasingly granular customization. Yoshi-P's 'yet' is a classic PR tactic — hinting at something without committing, keeping engagement high.

If Square Enix does add butt sliders, it could set a precedent for other games to adopt more body-slider options. The Evercold expansion may be the perfect opportunity to introduce this change, potentially boosting player retention.
